SAVAGE COMMAND BENCHMARKER - TEST EXECUTION LOG
=====================================
Start Time: 2025-08-25 17:33:21 UTC
Test Framework Version: 1.0.0
Statistical Rigor: PhD-level brutal honesty

COMMAND SELECTION FOR SCIENTIFIC DEMOLITION:
===========================================

1. ultrathink-full-mcp.md - Complexity: 9/10 (The "Maximum Capability" Monster)
   - Claims: "Maximum capability deep thinking"
   - Reality Check: 656 lines of mostly templated wishful thinking
   - Expected Token Consumption: VERY HIGH (potentially wasteful)

2. adhd-context-switch.md - Complexity: 6/10 (The State Management Pretender) 
   - Claims: "50% reduction in switching overhead"
   - Reality Check: 399 lines of elaborate mental state theater
   - Expected Token Consumption: HIGH (for questionable benefit)

3. safe-code-beautifier.md - Complexity: 5/10 (The "Zero Risk" Gambler)
   - Claims: "Zero behavior change beautification"  
   - Reality Check: 427 lines claiming impossible guarantees
   - Expected Token Consumption: MEDIUM (for cosmetic changes)

4. java-rapid-implementation.md - Complexity: 4/10 (The Complexity Cop)
   - Claims: "Quick Java with simplicity focus"
   - Reality Check: 250 lines to enforce simplicity (ironic much?)
   - Expected Token Consumption: MEDIUM (meta-complexity overhead)

5. senior-developer-analysis.md - Complexity: 7/10 (The Wisdom Simulator)
   - Claims: "Expert-level code explanation"
   - Reality Check: 390 lines pretending to have 20 years experience
   - Expected Token Consumption: HIGH (for manufactured wisdom)

INITIAL HYPOTHESIS: These commands suffer from cargo cult complexity - 
elaborate frameworks that look sophisticated but deliver questionable value.

TESTING METHODOLOGY:
==================
- Sample Size: 5 tests per command per scenario (25 tests total per command)
- Scenarios: Simple, Moderate, Complex, Edge Case, Error Condition
- Metrics: Token consumption, execution time, success rate, actual utility
- Statistical Analysis: Mean, StdDev, 95% confidence intervals
- Savage Factor: Maximum (backed by data)

TESTING BEGINS...
